summary: McKenzie describes posting a free classified ad on a popular teacher message board to promote his Bingo Card Creator software. Despite low expectations about anyone clicking through to find his software, he received a response within two hours. He notes the SEO benefit of the profile links and his willingness to provide hands-on customer support when the respondent needed help.
key_points: Free marketing channels like classified ads on niche community boards can yield surprisingly fast results
Even low-effort marketing tactics have secondary SEO benefits through profile links
Early-stage software businesses require hands-on customer support and willingness to 'handhold' users
money_quotes: I wasn't seriously expecting anyone to drill down two levels to get to my software, but hey, what could it have hurt?
They needed a little handholding, so I handheld.
key_insight: Free, low-effort marketing experiments in niche communities can generate customer contact surprisingly quickly, and early founders should be ready to provide personal support when they do.
